message:
test(config): malformed schema recovery with in-progress issue; close beads_rust-3h0h

config/mod.rs:
- Extract write_issue_jsonl() helper from write_single_issue_jsonl()
  to allow writing arbitrary Issue structs (not just id+title) to
  JSONL test fixtures.
- New test open_storage_with_cli_recovers_malformed_schema_db_with_
  in_progress_issue: creates a malformed blocked_issues_cache database,
  writes a fully populated in-progress Issue (with status, priority,
  issue_type, created_by, source_repo), then verifies that
  open_storage_with_cli() successfully recovers and all fields survive
  the recovery round-trip.

issues.jsonl:
- Close issue beads_rust-3h0h ("Auto-recover malformed
  blocked_issues_cache schema from JSONL") with close_reason
  documenting that recovery tests and live CLI verification pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"beads_rust-3g9e","title":"Integrate rich output into info and where commands","description":"## Commands: br info, br where

### Traffic Level: LOW
Project information commands.

### br info
```
╭─ Project Information ───────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Name          beads_rust                           │
│  Location      /home/user/projects/myapp/.beads     │
│  Prefix        beads_rust-                          │
│                                                     │
│  Database                                           │
│  ├── Path      .beads/beads.db                      │
│  ├── Size      2.4 MB                               │
│  └── Issues    156 total                            │
│                                                     │
│  JSONL                                              │
│  ├── Path      .beads/issues.jsonl                  │
│  ├── Size      1.8 MB                               │
│  └── Hash      abc123def456                         │
│                                                     │
│  Last Sync     2024-01-15 14:30 (2 hours ago)       │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### br where
Simple path output (useful for scripts):
```
/home/user/projects/myapp/.beads
```

With --verbose:
```
╭─ beads_rust Location ───────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Directory   /home/user/projects/myapp/.beads       │
│  Database    /home/user/projects/myapp/.beads/beads.db
│  JSONL       /home/user/projects/myapp/.beads/issues.jsonl
│  Config      /home/user/projects/myapp/.beads/config.yaml
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

### Testing Requirements

#### Unit Tests
```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn test_info_panel_formatting() {
        let info = ProjectInfo {
            name: "beads_rust".to_string(),
            location: "/home/user/.beads".into(),
            prefix: "beads_rust-".to_string(),
            db_path: "/home/user/.beads/beads.db".into(),
            db_size: 2_500_000,
            issue_count: 156,
            jsonl_path: "/home/user/.beads/issues.jsonl".into(),
            jsonl_size: 1_800_000,
            jsonl_hash: "abc123def456".to_string(),
            last_sync: Some(Utc::now()),
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_project_info(&info, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("beads_rust"));
        assert!(output.contains("156"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_info_size_formatting() {
        assert_eq!(format_bytes(2_500_000), "2.4 MB");
        assert_eq!(format_bytes(1_800_000), "1.7 MB");
        assert_eq!(format_bytes(500), "500 B");
        assert_eq!(format_bytes(1_500), "1.5 KB");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_where_simple_output() {
        let path = PathBuf::from("/home/user/.beads");
        let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
        let output = format_where(&path, false, &ctx);
        assert_eq!(output.trim(), "/home/user/.beads");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_where_verbose_output() {
        let paths = WherePaths {
            directory: "/home/user/.beads".into(),
            database: "/home/user/.beads/beads.db".into(),
            jsonl: "/home/user/.beads/issues.jsonl".into(),
            config: Some("/home/user/.beads/config.yaml".into()),
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
        let output = format_where_verbose(&paths, &ctx);
        assert!(output.contains("Directory"));
        assert!(output.contains("Database"));
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_json_mode_info() {
        let info = ProjectInfo {
            name: "test".to_string(),
            location: "/tmp/.beads".into(),
            prefix: "test-".to_string(),
            db_path: "/tmp/.beads/beads.db".into(),
            db_size: 1000,
            issue_count: 10,
            jsonl_path: "/tmp/.beads/issues.jsonl".into(),
            jsonl_size: 500,
            jsonl_hash: "abc".to_string(),
            last_sync: None,
        };
        let ctx = OutputContext::json();
        let output = format_project_info(&info, &ctx);
        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(parsed["name"], "test");
        assert_eq!(parsed["issue_count"], 10);
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_json_mode_where() {
        let path = PathBuf::from("/tmp/.beads");
        let ctx = OutputContext::json();
        let output = format_where(&path, false, &ctx);
        let parsed: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&output).unwrap();
        assert_eq!(parsed["path"], "/tmp/.beads");
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_relative_time_formatting() {
        let now = Utc::now();
        let two_hours_ago = now - chrono::Duration::hours(2);
        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(two_hours_ago, now), "2 hours ago");

        let yesterday = now - chrono::Duration::days(1);
        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(yesterday, now), "1 day ago");
    }
}
```

#### Integration Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_info_command_shows_project() {
    let (_storage, dir) = test_db_with_dir();
    init_test_project(&dir);

    let result = run_info_command(&dir);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    let output = result.unwrap();
    assert!(output.contains("test-")); // prefix
}

#[test]
fn test_where_command_returns_path() {
    let (_storage, dir) = test_db_with_dir();
    init_test_project(&dir);

    let result = run_where_command(&dir);
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    let path = result.unwrap();
    assert!(path.contains(".beads"));
}
```

#### E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Info/Where Commands ==="
setup_test_db

log_step "Initialize project"
br init --prefix test

log_step "Testing br info"
INFO_OUTPUT=$(br info)
log_debug "Info output: $INFO_OUTPUT"
if echo "$INFO_OUTPUT" | grep -q "test-"; then
    log_pass "br info shows prefix"
else
    log_fail "br info missing prefix"
    exit 1
fi

log_step "Testing br info --json"
INFO_JSON=$(br info --json)
log_debug "JSON: $INFO_JSON"
echo "$INFO_JSON" | jq -e '.prefix'
log_pass "br info JSON valid"

log_step "Testing br where"
WHERE_OUTPUT=$(br where)
log_debug "Where output: $WHERE_OUTPUT"
if echo "$WHERE_OUTPUT" | grep -q ".beads"; then
    log_pass "br where shows path"
else
    log_fail "br where missing .beads path"
    exit 1
fi

log_step "Testing br where --json"
WHERE_JSON=$(br where --json)
log_debug "JSON: $WHERE_JSON"
echo "$WHERE_JSON" | jq -e '.path'
log_pass "br where JSON valid"

log_pass "=== All info/where tests passed ==="
```

#### Logging Requirements
- Log info gathering: `debug!(db_size, issue_count, "Gathering project info")`
- Log path discovery: `trace!(beads_dir, "Discovered .beads directory")`","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:37:28.130960337Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T19:54:23.315466867Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T19:54:23.315366858Z","close_reason":"Verified implementation and tests.  {"id":"beads_rust-3gbd","title":"E2E tests: q (quick capture) command","description":"# E2E Tests for \`q\` (Quick Capture) Command

## Overview
The \`q\` command is a shorthand for rapid issue creation that returns only the issue ID (no verbose output). This enables scriptable workflows and fast capture.

## Commands to Test
- \`br q "Issue title"\` - Create issue, return ID only
- \`br q "Title" --type bug\` - Create with type
- \`br q "Title" --priority 1\` - Create with priority
- \`br q "Title" -t bug -p 0\` - Create with both

## Test Cases

### Success Paths (8 tests)
1. **q_creates_issue_returns_id_only** - Verify output is just the ID
2. **q_with_type_flag** - \`--type bug\` sets correct type
3. **q_with_priority_flag** - \`--priority 1\` sets correct priority
4. **q_with_all_flags** - Combine type + priority
5. **q_with_assignee** - \`--assignee me\` works
6. **q_with_labels** - \`--label urgent\` works
7. **q_output_is_valid_id** - ID matches bd-XXXX format
8. **q_issue_appears_in_list** - Created issue visible in list

### Error Cases (4 tests)
9. **q_without_init_fails** - Error before workspace init
10. **q_empty_title_fails** - Empty string rejected
11. **q_invalid_type_fails** - Unknown type rejected
12. **q_invalid_priority_fails** - Out of range priority rejected

### Scripting Integration (3 tests)
13. **q_output_usable_in_pipeline** - ID can be piped to other commands
14. **q_multiple_creates_unique_ids** - Rapid creates get unique IDs
15. **q_silent_mode_stderr** - No stderr output on success

## Logging Requirements
- Log command entry with all flags
- Log generated ID
- Log timing (start/end/duration)
- On error: log validation failure reason

## Test File Structure
\`\`\`
tests/e2e_quick_capture.rs
├── mod q_success_tests
│   ├── q_creates_issue_returns_id_only
│   ├── q_with_type_flag
│   └── ... (8 tests)
├── mod q_error_tests
│   ├── q_without_init_fails
│   └── ... (4 tests)
└── mod q_scripting_tests
    ├── q_output_usable_in_pipeline
    └── ... (3 tests)
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file: tests/e2e_quick_capture.rs
- [ ] 15 test functions minimum
- [ ] All tests log entry/exit with timing
- [ ] Verify ID format consistency","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldHarbor","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:18:35.967230065Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T18:28:54.968320094Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T18:28:54.968320094Z","close_reason":"E2E tests implemented in tests/e2e_quick_capture.rs (19 tests incl ID format and pipeline checks)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3gbd","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-oxmd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-3hl","title":"Last-touched tracking (.beads/last-touched)","description":"# Last-Touched Tracking (.beads/last-touched)

## Purpose
Maintain the most recently touched issue ID for commands that omit IDs (update/close/show).

## Storage
- File: `.beads/last-touched`
- Permissions: `0600`
- Content: single ID + newline

## Behavior
- `SetLastTouchedID(id)`: best-effort (ignore errors).
- `GetLastTouchedID()`: returns empty on missing/invalid file.
- `ClearLastTouched()`: best-effort delete.

## Which Commands Set It
- `create`: set to new issue ID.
- `update`: set to **first updated** ID.
- `show`: set to **first shown** ID (or routed ID).
- `close`: **does not** update last-touched.

## Acceptance Criteria
- File permissions correct (0600).
- Missing file is non-fatal.

## Tests
- Set/get/clear behaviors.
- `close` does not update last-touched.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:03:32.570162726Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T13:56:39.497856533Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T13:56:39.497856533Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
